new_classes.definition_en: A distillation and blending process by which blended malt whisky is produced by combining two or more single malt whiskies obtained at different distilleries.
A quality borne by a spirit that is a blended malt whisky, i.e. produced by blending two or more single malt whiskies distilled at different distilleries.
A quality borne by a spirit that is a Scotch whisky, i.e. whisky produced in Scotland in accordance with the applicable legal requirements.

new_classes.label_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky, in containers holding ≤ 2 l (CN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.label_de: Blended Malt Scotch Whisky in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l (KN 2208 30 41)
new_classes.definition_en: Blended malt Scotch whisky — produced by blending two or more single malt Scotch whiskies distilled at different distilleries — presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN code 2208 30 41.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the four main discriminating criteria for CN 2208 30 41: (1) the product is a blended malt whisky (BlendedMaltWhiskyQuality), (2) it is Scotch (ScotchWhiskyOriginQuality), (3) it is produced by the blending of two or more single malt whiskies from different distilleries (BlendedMaltWhiskyProduction process), and (4) it is presented in containers holding ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer). The container size threshold is the key discriminator between 2208 30 41 (≤ 2 l) and its sibling 2208 30 49 (> 2 l). Coverage is slightly reduced (0.85) because (a) the sister code 2208 30 49 is not yet modelled so an explicit disjointness axiom on container size cannot be added in the same call, and (b) the minimum alcoholic strength requirement for whiskies (typically ≥ 40% vol under EU spirits regulation) is referenced by the broader 2208 heading context but is not explicitly encoded here, as the relevant alcoholic strength quality class for < 80% vol spirits is present in the TBox only at a high level (UndenaturedEthylAlcoholLessThan80VolQuality) and whisky-specific strength thresholds are not part of the provided legal note text for this code.
